message:
fix(patterns): [HIGH] canonicalize .ft/patterns at the directory boundary (ft-6mxfs)

## The vector

ft-fbee5 closed symlink-file escapes *inside* `.ft/patterns/`. But the
parent path was still `root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns")` with no
canonicalization, so a hostile repo could ship

    .ft/patterns -> /etc

(a top-level symlink on the directory itself) and escape from one level
up. `discover_packs_from_dir` happily iterated the target; the per-file
`sandbox_resolve` inside it used the symlinked-to path as its own root,
so every file under `/etc` looked in-sandbox. Parse errors on those
files leaked fragments back through `tracing::warn!` — the same
exfiltration primitive ft-fbee5 closed, one layer up.

## Fix

New `sandbox_resolve_dir(candidate, root) -> Result<Option<PathBuf>>`
helper in patterns.rs:

  * Ok(None) when the candidate doesn't exist (the 99% case — most
    repos don't ship `.ft/patterns/`). Caller skips discovery silently,
    matching the old `discover_packs_from_dir` early-return on
    `!dir.exists()`.
  * Ok(Some(canonical)) when the candidate canonicalizes inside `root`.
    Caller walks the CANONICAL path, closing the TOCTOU window where
    a second symlink could be swapped in between the check and the walk.
  * Err(InvalidRule) when the candidate exists but resolves outside
    `root` — the escape case. Error cites ft-6mxfs so audits don't
    get lost.

`load_packs_from_config` now routes `ws_dir` through the helper and
skips discovery if it returns None or refuses it if it returns an
escape error. The per-file sandbox gate inside `discover_packs_from_dir`
is untouched — it still closes the ft-fbee5 case. Defense in depth:
the parent-level check in the loader + the leaf-level check in
discovery.

Helper is intentionally centralized. The same pattern (join a
workspace-relative `.ft/**` subpath, then walk it) will recur for
workflows, policy files, other future loaders; they should reuse
`sandbox_resolve_dir` rather than reinventing the check.

## Tests (4 new)

  * sandbox_resolve_dir_missing_returns_none — missing dir → Ok(None),
    no error, no panic.
  * sandbox_resolve_dir_inside_returns_canonical — real in-sandbox dir
    → Ok(Some(canonical)) that starts with the canonical root.
  * sandbox_resolve_dir_rejects_toplevel_symlink_escape — .ft/patterns
    symlink to an outside temp dir → InvalidRule, message cites
    "outside sandbox root" + ft-6mxfs.
  * load_packs_from_config_refuses_symlinked_patterns_dir — end-to-end:
    even with a loadable decoy pack on the other end of the symlink,
    load_packs_from_config rejects instead of silently reading it.

## Verification

Lib compiles clean:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p1-orch-target \
    cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib \
    --no-default-features --features asupersync-runtime
  → Finished (5 pre-existing warnings, 0 errors).

Full `cargo test --lib` is blocked by unrelated compile errors in
diagnostic.rs (missing DiagnosticOptions.include_full_cwd field) and
retry.rs (non-exhaustive match on Error) from concurrent-agent
work. The edits in this commit do not touch those modules and the
added tests have been manually reviewed for syntactic correctness —
they use only public/internal patterns.rs API and `fs::symlink` /
`tempfile::tempdir` primitives already exercised by the pre-existing
`discover_packs_rejects_symlink_escape` test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
